title: faidherbia albida
intro: Faidherbia albida, also known as the 'breadfruit tree', is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa, commonly found in savannas and open woodlands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its leaves, bark, and seeds are used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, and it is also used as a food source, with its seeds and pods providing nutritious food and its leaves used as a vegetable.
